original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
original_registry_entry.version: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism 2011;96(2):273–288; DOI 10.1210/jc.2010-1692
original_registry_entry.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing, imaging and referral policy governs local use
original_registry_entry.population: Nonpregnant and pregnant people with elevated prolactin, suspected medication-related hyperprolactinaemia or prolactinoma-related context.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Endocrine evaluation of an elevated prolactin result and longitudinal specialist review.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for confirming result context, reviewing physiological, medicine, renal, thyroid and pituitary causes, selected imaging context and clinician follow-up. It does not infer a cause or authorise treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.3 — diagnosis and result verification
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording serum prolactin result context, repeat or macroprolactin context when clinician-selected and symptoms relevant to an elevated result.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1–3.3 — cause, medicine and imaging evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of medicine, renal, thyroid, physiological and pituitary context and already performed imaging without assigning a cause.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 4.1–6.7 — follow-up and special contexts
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led symptom, laboratory, imaging, adverse-effect, pregnancy and follow-up review. Medicine names, doses and treatment instructions are excluded.

title: Diagnosis and Treatment of Hyperprolactinemia: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing, imaging and referral policy governs local use
population: Nonpregnant and pregnant people with elevated prolactin, suspected medication-related hyperprolactinaemia or prolactinoma-related context.
intended_setting: Endocrine evaluation of an elevated prolactin result and longitudinal specialist review.
